writeup: The Buffalo Bills closed the book on their 2026 offseason program on June 12, capping it with the signing of fourth-round offensive tackle Jude Bowry — the final member of their draft class to ink a deal — as the franchise turns its attention fully toward training camp. With mandatory minicamp now in the rearview mirror, the spotlight shifted to evaluating a loaded and revamped roster operating under first-year head coach Joe Brady, whose new-look staff has energized Western New York heading into a critical season. Roster transactions continued to ripple post-minicamp, as RB Desmond Reid reverted to injured reserve and DB Dorian Strong was formally placed on the reserve/NFI list, trimming the 90-man roster ahead of the July break. Analysts across the league pegged the Bills as a top AFC contender, with NFL.com ranking Buffalo first among franchises most likely to claim their first Lombardi Trophy, buoyed by Josh Allen's elite play and a retooled defense under coordinator Jim Leonhard. Between underrated sleeper profiles, minicamp standouts like rookie edge rusher T.J. Parker, and a daunting 2026 schedule preview, Bills coverage on June 12 painted a picture of a team under genuine championship pressure.
